response:
THE LAW OF MOORE, FORMULATED BY GORDON ECKERT MOORE IN 1965, PREDICTS THAT THE NUMBER OF TRANSISTORS ON A MICROPROCESSOR WILL DOUBLE EVERY TWO YEARS. THIS TRENDS HAS BEEN A DRIVING FORCE BEHIND THE EXPONENTIAL GROWTH OF COMPUTING POWER AND TECHNOLOGICAL ADVANCEMENTS. HOWEVER, AS TECHNOLOGY HAS PROGRESSED, THE PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS OF SILICON BASED TECHNOLOGY HAVE BECOME MORE APPARENT. FACTORS SUCH AS HEAT DISSIPATION, QUANTUM TUNNELLING, AND THE COST OF MANUFACTURING HAVE MADE IT INCREASINGLY DIFFICULT TO CONTINUE THIS RATE OF GROWTH.

***
WHILE THE LAW OF MOORE MAY NOT BE A STRICTLY MATHEMATICAL LAW, IT HAS BEEN A USEFUL PREDICTION FOR INDUSTRY PLANNING AND INVESTMENT. MANY EXPERTS BELIEVE THAT THE LAW WILL EVENTUALLY CEASE TO HOLD TRUE AS WE REACH THE PHYSICAL LIMITS OF SILICON TECHNOLOGY. ALTERNATIVE MATERIALS AND NEW COMPUTING PARADIGMS, SUCH AS QUANTUM COMPUTING AND NEURAL NETWORKS, ARE BEING EXPLORRED TO EXTEND THE LIFE OF MOORE'S LAW. HOWEVER, IT IS DIFFICULT TO PREDICT EXACTLY WHEN THIS WILL HAPPEN, AS IT DEPENDS ON A VARIETY OF FACTORS INCLUDING SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERY AND INDUSTRIAL INNOVATION. TECHNOLOGY DO
